knobs.audit: reconcile register-lifetime verdict against Tensile's real envelope

isa: Tensile MAX VGPR=256 (HW allows pipelined 128x128); build_gemm_lds2 8-wave W4x2T2x4 PLRAB fits 188 VGPR PIPELINED
local_diagnostic: whole-prefill +0.5% (8-wave deep pipeline); still ~4% below Tensile
stop_reason: deep pipeline expressible+fits but +0.5% only; residual is fine instruction scheduling (asm scheduler), below the template
learned_rule: ADVERSARIAL CORRECTION: the prior 'prefill register-pool HW limit' was RETRACTED. Tensile fits a pipelined 128x128 GEMM in 256 VGPR; build_gemm_lds2 CAN express the matching deep pipeline via the 8-wave W4x2T2x4 layout (acc=64, PLRAB 188 VGPR, PIPELINED, correct) -- my prior analysis used the wrong 4-wave 4x4 layout. BUT matching the pipeline STRUCTURE recovers only +0.5% whole-prefill; the residual ~4% is FINE INSTRUCTION SCHEDULING (waitcnt/WGM/cadence/SIA1), below the build_gemm_lds2 template -> needs an asm instruction scheduler. CURRENT_LDS2_REPRESENTATION_EXHAUSTED, Tensile path unresolved (not a HW limit).

knobs.capability: instruction IR + intra-region dependency DAG over build_gemm_lds2's list[Inst]; pure-compute reorder within memory+fence-delimited regions

oracle: Tensile cycle-level schedule (consumer-only waitcnt, SIA1 cadence, WGM8)
correctness: identity byte-identical; 554 insts legally permuted still rmse 2.07e-4 across PLRA/DBUF/8-wave-PLRAB configs
isa: exact register def/use decoded from instruction encoding (OpType-aware: VGPR/unified-src/SGPR/inline; async load dest, wmma src2==vdst RMW)
local_diagnostic: extra/qk_asm_scheduler_inc0_test.py P1-P6 (byte-identical / decode-coverage / DAG-legality / layout-preserved / identity-correct / legal-reorder-correct)
authority_benchmark: non-promotion (Inc 0 is a faithfulness proof, not a speed claim)
stop_reason: Inc 0 complete; reorder-for-speed (consumer-only waitcnt + async-load counter model) is Inc 1
learned_rule: An asm instruction scheduler for the prefill GEMM is buildable on build_gemm_lds2's list[Inst]: register def/use decodes EXACTLY from the encoding (operands+_raw bitfields, OpType-classified). Inc 0 keystone = a FAITHFUL IR+DAG, proven by byte-identical identity reorder + a heavy LEGAL reorder that still computes correctly on GPU. Key soundness lesson: async loads write their dest at DRAIN time not issue time, so the synchronous register model cannot move memory ops -- anchor them and defer to the wait-counter model (Inc 1). The proof caught a RMW self-edge and an MMU-faulting async-load hoist; a reorder test that runs on hardware is the real faithfulness gate.

knobs.capability: async-load wait-counter (s_waitcnt) model: decode/encode, verify_wait_correct gate, wait_constraints audit, recompute_waits_inplace, fence_only region mode (default off)

correctness: Q1-Q6 PASS gfx1100; recompute-in-place + Inc-0 reorder both rmse 2.07e-4
isa: vmcnt/lgkmcnt issue-order retirement model: wait for op at issue-position s needs cnt<=issued-1-s; barrier=>lgkm0, endpgm=>both0
local_diagnostic: extra/qk_asm_scheduler_inc1_test.py (audit / recompute / verify / discriminate / compose / necessary-not-sufficient)
authority_benchmark: non-promotion (capability + audit; no speed claim -- waits already minimal)
stop_reason: standalone relax ~free (slack=1); cross-motion exposes an RDNA3 hardware-spacing hazard beyond register+wait modeling -> Inc 2 hazard recognizer
learned_rule: The s_waitcnt wait-counter model (vmcnt/lgkmcnt, issue-order retirement) is buildable and is the soundness gate for memory-op motion. TWO honest findings: (1) build_gemm_lds2's hand-placed full drains are ALREADY minimal (relaxable slack=1, a perf-irrelevant prologue scalar load) so consumer-only relaxation buys ~nothing standalone -- the lever needs reordering; (2) wait-correctness is NECESSARY-NOT-SUFFICIENT: a register-legal (0 missing DAG edges) AND wait-correct fence_only reorder still computes wrong in the prologue (per-piece reorders all correct; only tight cross-motion breaks) = an RDNA3 hardware-spacing/scoreboard hazard. So an asm scheduler needs THREE gates -- register DAG (Inc 0) + wait-counter (Inc 1) + a hardware hazard recognizer (Inc 2); the first two are necessary but not sufficient.

knobs.capability: loop-entry (branch-target) boundary gate + critical-path latency-aware schedule over fence_only regions

correctness: fence_only cross-motion byte-identical across default_PLRA/kv_halved/DBUF1/8wave_PLRAB, asap(167-310 mem moved)+critical modes, rmse 2.07e-4
isa: loop-entry = backward-branch target from signed simm16+byte offsets; ISA-confirmed s_delay_alu is PERF-ONLY (RDNA3 interlocks VALU/VMEM, no spacing-correctness hazard)
local_diagnostic: extra/qk_asm_scheduler_inc2_test.py R1/R2 correctness PASS; R3 clean clock-pinned isolated timing
stop_reason: pure reordering is perf-neutral on the hand-tuned kernel; residual needs waitcnt-relocation/cross-iteration pipelining (Inc 3), not reordering a fixed instruction set
learned_rule: CORRECTION of Inc 1: a register-legal AND wait-correct fence_only reorder that computed wrong was NOT an RDNA3 hardware-spacing hazard -- it crossed the LOOP-ENTRY (backward-branch target), an unmodeled control-flow boundary. Fix = branch_target_indices as region boundaries; then cross-motion is byte-identical-correct. ISA research confirms s_delay_alu is PERFORMANCE-ONLY on RDNA3 (hardware interlocks VALU/VMEM register deps; reorder cannot corrupt values via spacing) -- only s_waitcnt/control-flow/the VALU->SGPR->VMEM 5-wait-state hazard are correctness-relevant. A critical-path latency-aware reorder over the now-sound substrate is PERF-NEUTRAL (+/-<1%, ~60 TFLOPS clock-pinned): pure instruction reordering does NOT recover the prefill->Tensile residual (hardware scoreboard + hand-tuning already hide in-region latency; regions are full-drain-wait-bounded). The remaining reorder-class lever is waitcnt RELOCATION (interleave ds_load/wmma + per-consumer waits), which changes the instruction set -- Inc 3. The 3 asm-scheduler correctness gates: register DAG + wait-counter + branch-target boundaries.

knobs.capability: waitcnt relocation: strip compute-block full-drain lgkm(0), issue WMMAs frag-ready-first, insert per-WMMA minimal lgkmcnt; branch-offset fixup (capture_branch_targets/fix_branches non-mutating)

correctness: byte-correct (rmse<=3e-4) + verify_wait_correct across plain/DBUF1/PLRA/kv_halved + NBLK 16/32/128; non-mutating
isa: fragment load at issue-position s among P producers -> lgkmcnt<=P-1-s; inserting waits requires branch simm16 recompute by target identity
local_diagnostic: extra/qk_asm_scheduler_inc3_test.py S1/S2 correctness PASS; S3 clean clock-pinned isolated timing
stop_reason: config-dependent win (DBUF1 +6% / PLRA +2% / kv -4%); modest+mixed isolated signal, promotion needs per-role gating + whole-prefill confirmation; not crossed
learned_rule: Waitcnt RELOCATION (changing the instruction SET, unlike Inc 2's neutral pure reorder) is the FIRST asm-scheduler lever that moves the needle: removing the compute-block full-drain lgkm(0) and inserting per-WMMA minimal lgkmcnt (WMMAs frag-ready-first) overlaps WMMA compute with LDS-load latency. Clean clock-pinned isolated: DBUF1 ~+6% (reproduced), PLRA route ~+2%, kv_halved ~-4% REGRESSION. Config-dependent: the win is recovering EXPOSED LDS latency, so low-occupancy (DBUF1, 2x LDS) gains and high-occupancy small-N (kv_halved, latency already wave-hidden) loses. Inserting waits needs branch-offset recompute (by target Inst identity, non-mutating shallow-copy -- mutating the shared branch corrupts the identity stream). verify_wait_correct caught a real double-emit WAW-across-substeps bug. ARC SUMMARY (Inc0-3): the prefill->Tensile residual is NOT recoverable by instruction reordering; only mild config-dependent gains (~+2% route) from wait-restructuring -- vendored Tensile remains the path to parity.

knobs.integration: PREFILL_GEMM_RELOC route flag (default off, waves_n==2/non-kv roles); kv-regression occupancy breakdown

correctness: route relocation byte-correct (proven isolated); whole-prefill runs clean
isa: relocation benefit = exposed LDS latency ∝ 1/occupancy; cost = extra-waitcnt overhead
local_diagnostic: causal occupancy sweep (same kv kernel, vary LDS): relocation Δ = +0.08%@4WG/CU, -3.03%@2WG, +4.26%@1WG
stop_reason: isolated +2% consumed by in-model integration (GEMM is a fraction; real-shape occupancy higher; 0.7% noise floor); whole-prefill net = none -> default-off, arc complete
learned_rule: Chasing the Inc-3 +2%: (A) the kv_halved -4% regression is OCCUPANCY -- causal sweep (same kernel, only LDS alloc varied) shows relocation Δ = +0.08%@4WG/CU -> -3.03%@2WG -> +4.26%@1WG, i.e. benefit=LDS-latency-overlap ∝ 1/occupancy, cost=extra-waitcnt overhead (~const); net flips sign with occupancy. (B) Wired into the route (PREFILL_GEMM_RELOC, waves_n==2 only) and measured clock-pinned synced WHOLE-PREFILL: net effect = NONE (reloc vs baseline ~0.5% < 0.7% run-to-run noise). The isolated +2% does NOT transfer in-model (GEMM is a fraction of prefill; real-role occupancy is higher than the 4096^3 probe). Ships default-off. ARC CLOSE (asm scheduler Inc0-3): the prefill->Tensile residual is NOT recoverable by ANY instruction-scheduling transform of the current kernel -- only vendored Tensile or a structurally different emit (deeper DepthU/cross-iteration pipelining) closes it. Reconfirms the standing lesson: isolated kernel wins don't transfer.

knobs.validation: full-8B clock-pinned 3-repeat whole-prefill A/B/C/D + isolated kv occupancy sweep

correctness: all routes run clean; relocation rmse 2.08e-4
local_diagnostic: whole-prefill@4096 3-repeat medians: graph 2994, reloc_w1 2984, reloc_w4 2988, tensile 2800
stop_reason: graph-GEMM already beats Tensile route in-model by ~6% at every context; relocation washes to noise; whole-prefill is INSENSITIVE to GEMM-kernel speed (relocation +2-4% kernel -> 0% whole; Tensile faster-kernel -> SLOWER whole)
learned_rule: VALIDATION overturns the prefill->Tensile premise. Full-8B clock-pinned 3-repeat whole-prefill: the graph-GEMM route already BEATS the Tensile route by ~6% at EVERY context (graph 3720/3635/3399/2997 vs tensile 3506/3429/3200/2793 @512/1024/2048/4096), despite Tensile's isolated GEMM being ~10% faster (66 vs 60 TFLOPS) -- the throughput edge is eaten by in-model integration (Tensile-layout/transpose kernels, weaker fusion). Relocation washes to <0.3% whole-prefill (within 0.13% noise) though it is a real +2-4% isolated occupancy-driven lever. KEY: whole-prefill is INSENSITIVE to GEMM-kernel-speed changes in BOTH directions -> the prefill bottleneck is NOT the GEMM kernel emit; it is integration + attention (the @512->@4096 decay 3720->2997). Retires 'graph-GEMM ~99.5% of Tensile, close the gap'. Any GEMM-kernel structural-emit change is very unlikely to move whole-prefill.

knobs.change: route default PLRA(substep-prefetch) -> DBUF(cross-iteration double-buffer); global emit knobs PREFILL_GEMM_{DBUF,BK,PLRA,PLRAB,LEANADDR}

isa: DBUF = prefetch next K-block global loads into other LDS buffer while computing current (full block-level pipeline, removes inner barrier); 2x LDS -> lower occupancy
local_diagnostic: stress study 8 candidates x 6 repeats x ctx512..8192 + interleaved paired A/B (drift-cancelled)
stop_reason: shipped: byte-identical + significant + reversible; blocked deeper structural candidates are VGPR-walled (bk64=268, plrab4x4=300, acc-partition, full reg-pool)
learned_rule: STRUCTURAL emit changes CAN move whole-prefill where SCHEDULING-only cannot. Stress study (full 8B, 6 repeats, ctx 512..8192, significance-gated, interleaved-paired confirmation): swapping the route's substep-prefetch PLRA for cross-iteration DOUBLE-BUFFER (DBUF) = +2.84% +/-0.11% whole-prefill@4096 BYTE-IDENTICAL (logit max_abs_diff=0), significant at every context; DBUF+relocation=+3.87% (reloc transfers now because DBUF lowers occupancy into the exposed-LDS-latency regime). Relocation ALONE = +0.1% (noise, non-transfer) -- confirms scheduling-only doesn't transfer. Root cause: DBUF's full block-level pipelining beats PLRA's substep-only prefetch IN-MODEL; the old plra=1 default was set on ISOLATED kernel benchmarks (the isolated->integrated reversal, AGAIN). Default flipped dbuf=1,plra=0 (reversible). Deeper structural candidates are VGPR-walled (DepthU bk64=268>256, PLRAB-4x4=300, acc-partition 2x128>256, full reg-pool HW-limited). LESSON: when isolated and in-model disagree, the route default must be chosen on IN-MODEL whole-prefill, and STRUCTURAL (not scheduling) emit changes are where the real in-model gains live.
